label: Object storage, sharding & checkpoints

node.label: Object storage, sharding & checkpoints
node.definition: Object stores, distributed filesystems, shards, and checkpoint formats persist datasets, weights, optimizer state, embeddings, traces, and artifacts.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Object storage, sharding & checkpoints is to connect Distributed systems & networking, Databases & information retrieval to downstream capabilities including Workflow, cluster & job orchestration, Model servers & inference runtimes, Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps.
examples: substrate for Workflow, cluster & job orchestration
substrate for Model servers & inference runtimes
substrate for Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps
limitations: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Distributed systems & networking, Databases & information retrieval.

dependencies.rationale: Object storage, sharding & checkpoints turns Distributed systems & networking into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
Object storage, sharding & checkpoints turns Databases & information retrieval into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
Object storage, sharding & checkpoints turns Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
